title: load_host()'s silent fallback to Claude defaults fail-opens the entire write-gate surface on Codex
evidence: hostapi.load_host() catches every exception from executing _host.py and returns a bare Host() (Claude defaults) with no warn()/breadcrumb (`except Exception: return Host()`). Reproduced: feeding a Codex apply_patch payload to the base Host yields `[{'file_path': '', 'kind': 'write', ...}]` — file_path is empty, so classify_protected('') hits nothing and pre-write allows the patch, including one rewriting `.codearbiter/overrides.log`. The 'degrades to today's behavior instead of breaking' rationale (hostapi.py:180-182) is correct only for the ca plugin, where the fallback IS the right host; on ca-codex a corrupted/missing _host.py silently swaps in the WRONG host: all writes unguarded, project_root consulting a leaked CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (the exact wrong-project bug _host.py:21-25 exists to prevent), has_statusline/has_read_tool wrongly True.
impact: One syntax error or a partial install of ca-codex converts every enforcement gate to a no-op with zero signal — silent-un-enforcement, the failure class commit e19778c already exhibited once. The base-class-IS-Claude asymmetry means the failure mode is invisible on the host where it is harmless and catastrophic on the host where it is not.
recommendation: Make the fallback host-aware and loud: at minimum emit a warn() breadcrumb on fallback; better, ship a one-line expected-host sentinel (e.g. hooks.json or a HOST_NAME constant vendored per plugin) and fail CLOSED (exit 2) in enforcement entries when the loaded host's name does not match the plugin's expected host. Add a doctor check for the mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: A ca-codex install with a syntactically broken _host.py blocks (or loudly warns on) tool calls instead of silently allowing them
load_host() fallback leaves a visible breadcrumb (stderr or gate-events.log)
